title: ceratopteris richardii
intro: Ceratopteris richardii, also known as the water sprite or water fern, is a species of aquatic fern native to Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, including its antifungal, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as a natural diuretic. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and made into teas, infusions, or capsules, and is also used in traditional folk remedies for its purported medicinal properties.
